title: copilot.* IPC surface + preload bridge
predecessor: M33-T4 shipped 2026-04-16, commit e672973 (CopilotAnalyzerService + CopilotEventTrigger + migration 0012 + 14 unit tests; 1060 → 1074)
goal: Wire the four Phase 5 §8.6 copilot.* IPC channels end-to-end across the TeamXApi preload bridge so the renderer (M34) can subscribe to the analyzer's output without touching the analyzer directly. Thin IPC surface on top of the T4 service: `copilot.insights` (paginated listActive), `copilot.dismiss` (proxy to insightsRepo.dismiss + emit bus event for invalidation), `copilot.ask` (deferred to T6 — IPC slot is registered but routes through agentic loop), `copilot.configure` (test-only manual-tick IPC for E2E, feature-flagged OFF in production). All channels typed end-to-end via TeamXApi in packages/shared-types/src/ipc.ts. Handlers in apps/desktop/src/main/ipc/copilot-handlers.ts mirroring the T5 pattern established in previous milestones (ipc/rag-handlers.ts is the canonical precedent).
acceptance: NEW apps/desktop/src/main/ipc/copilot-handlers.ts — thin handler factory matching the rag-handlers.ts pattern. Exports buildCopilotHandlers(deps) returning { copilotInsights, copilotDismiss, copilotConfigure }
copilot.insights handler — wraps copilotInsightsRepo.listActive with pagination (limit + cursor); returns CopilotInsight[] wire type + nextCursor
copilot.dismiss handler — proxies to copilotInsightsRepo.dismiss(id); emits 'copilot.dismissed' bus event for renderer React Query invalidation (per invariant #11)

copilot.configure handler — test-only manual-tick IPC; resolves when analyzer.tick(companyId, { reason: 'manual' }) completes. Feature-flagged via isTestMode() — production throws an error when called
copilot.ask IPC slot registered but delegates to T6 (returns { runId, threadId } shape matching AgenticLoopService.start via the complex_request path); T5 ships the STUB only

packages/shared-types/src/ipc.ts — TeamXApi extended with `copilot: { insights, dismiss, configure, ask }` namespace. Matching wire types (CopilotInsightListArgs / CopilotInsightListResult / CopilotDismissArgs / CopilotConfigureArgs / CopilotAskArgs / CopilotAskResult) exported from ipc.ts or a new copilot.ts module

packages/shared-types/src/copilot.ts (NEW) — wire CopilotInsight type (id, companyId, category, severity, title, detail, actionSuggestion, actionIntent, actionEntitiesJson, dismissedAt, createdAt, expiresAt) matching the repo row shape. Mirrors packages/shared-types/src/vault.ts pattern

apps/desktop/src/preload/index.ts — window.teamx.copilot namespace exposes invoke-based wrappers for all 4 channels (insights, dismiss, configure, ask). IPC channel registration in main/ipc/register.ts adds the 4 channel names
apps/desktop/src/main/ipc/register.ts — 4 new channel registrations via registerIpcHandlers extension

apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts composition root — buildCopilotHandlers wired with deps { copilotInsightsRepo, copilotAnalyzerServiceInstance, bus, isTestMode } and returned object spread into registerIpcHandlers(...) alongside the other handler modules

Unit tests +8 — copilot-handlers.test.ts with 2 tests per channel (happy path + error path): insights happy+invalid-companyId, dismiss happy+unknown-id, configure happy+production-throw, ask-stub happy+missing-text. Target 1074 → 1082
Typecheck clean across all 6 workspace packages
Atomic commit: feat(m33): M33 T5 — copilot.* IPC surface + preload bridge
Ledger commit: chore(loki): M33 T5 — commit ledger (<sha>)
filesTouched: packages/shared-types/src/copilot.ts (NEW) — CopilotInsight wire type + CopilotInsight* IPC arg/result types
packages/shared-types/src/ipc.ts — extend TeamXApi.copilot namespace with 4 channels
apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts — composition root wiring (buildCopilotHandlers import + instantiation + pass to registerIpcHandlers)
notes: Mirror the rag-handlers.ts pattern exactly — it's the canonical precedent for a thin IPC handler module that wraps a service + repo. Read that file first and match its shape (buildXHandlers factory + closure-captured deps + one-function-per-channel).

copilot.configure is TEST-ONLY in T5 — production callers should get a clear error message saying 'copilot.configure is a test-only IPC; use settings.setCopilot from T7 for production configuration'. The E2E spec in T9 needs SOME way to force a manual tick without waiting for the 5-minute scheduled interval; that's why this channel exists.

copilot.ask is a STUB in T5 — full implementation is T6 (routes through AgenticLoopService.start with employeeId = system-copilot). T5 ships the IPC slot + TeamXApi type + preload bridge so T6 can be purely additive. The handler in T5 can throw 'Not implemented — T6' or return a canned runId/threadId for now; prefer throwing so no caller accidentally relies on the stub.

Wire-type discipline — CopilotInsight in shared-types MUST be a distinct type from CopilotInsightRow in the repo. The wire type is JSON-safe (dismissedAt: number | null, not Date | null). Mirror how Vault / Tickets / Goals separate wire types from Drizzle row types.

Bus event for dismissal — emit 'copilot.dismissed' (NEW EventType literal) + CopilotDismissedPayload with { insightId, dismissedAt } so the renderer can invalidate its cache. This adds one EventType; register in the same events.ts block as the 3 existing copilot.* events. Audit invariant #11 alignment.

Pagination shape — mirror M14's events.list (cursor-based). limit defaults to 50, cap 100. `nextCursor` is either null (end) or a numeric timestamp (createdAt of the last row). Renderer uses infinite-query pattern.
T5 is boilerplate-heavy but low-risk — estimate 30-45 min. T6 (copilot.ask routing + query_copilot_insights tool) is the next biggest surface after T4.
No migration in T5 (schema is stable from T4). No composition-root service wiring beyond the IPC hookup. No test seam changes (analyzer still uses T4's test-mode resolveComplete).
